name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 01.12.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 01.12.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: As on November 28, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 393.07 lakh ha (including 77.06 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 62.49 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 357.73 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: As on November 28, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 393.07 lakh ha (including 77.06 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 62.49 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 357.73 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As  on  28.11.2025,  64.40%  of  area  has  been  harvested  for  Kharif  crops  all  over  India. Approximately, 60% area of Pulses mainly in Uradbean (95%), Moongbean (99%) & Mothbean (100%);  94.46%  area  of  Oilseeds  majorly  in  Groundnut  (94.83%),    Soyabean  (99.99%)& Sesamum (92.28%); 89.92% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (91.04%) & Bajra (97.77%) and 65% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.
texts.text:  As  on  28.11.2025,  64.40%  of  area  has  been  harvested  for  Kharif  crops  all  over  India. Approximately, 60% area of Pulses mainly in Uradbean (95%), Moongbean (99%) & Mothbean (100%);  94.46%  area  of  Oilseeds  majorly  in  Groundnut  (94.83%),    Soyabean  (99.99%)& Sesamum (92.28%); 89.92% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (91.04%) & Bajra (97.77%) and 65% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be below normal 2-4°C over most parts of  the  country  except  northeast India  and  Western  Himalayan  Region, where these are likely to be above normal by 1-3°C during the week .
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be below normal 2-4°C over most parts of  the  country  except  northeast India  and  Western  Himalayan  Region, where these are likely to be above normal by 1-3°C during the week .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  November  30  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  23%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East & Northeast India: 7%; (ii) Northwest India: 75%; (iii) Central India: 44%; and South Peninsula: 9%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  November  30  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  23%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East & Northeast India: 7%; (ii) Northwest India: 75%; (iii) Central India: 44%; and South Peninsula: 9%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The Cyclonic Storm Ditwah over coastal Sri Lanka and adjoining southwest Bay of Bengal, heavy rainfall likely over north Tamil Nadu & Odisha on 01 st  December; Coastal Andhra Pradesh,  Yanam  &  Rayalaseema  on  01 st   &  02 nd   December;  over  Telangana  on  01 st December 2025.
texts.text:  The Cyclonic Storm Ditwah over coastal Sri Lanka and adjoining southwest Bay of Bengal, heavy rainfall likely over north Tamil Nadu & Odisha on 01 st  December; Coastal Andhra Pradesh,  Yanam  &  Rayalaseema  on  01 st   &  02 nd   December;  over  Telangana  on  01 st December 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Western  disturbance  as  an  upper  air  cyclonic  circulation  over  North  Haryana  & neighbourhood.
texts.text:  The  Western  disturbance  as  an  upper  air  cyclonic  circulation  over  North  Haryana  & neighbourhood.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Thunderstorm with lightning very likely over Tamil Nadu, North Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam and Rayalaseema during 01 st -03 rd  December; over South Interior Karnataka on 01 st     December;  over  Kerala  &  Mahe  during  02 nd   &  03 rd   December;  North  interior Karnataka 01 st   December, 2025 .
texts.text:  Thunderstorm with lightning very likely over Tamil Nadu, North Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam and Rayalaseema during 01 st -03 rd  December; over South Interior Karnataka on 01 st     December;  over  Kerala  &  Mahe  during  02 nd   &  03 rd   December;  North  interior Karnataka 01 st   December, 2025 .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall rainfall activity is likely to below normal over most parts of India.
texts.text:  Overall rainfall activity is likely to below normal over most parts of India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Dense fog conditions very likely to prevail during early morning hours in isolated pockets of Himachal Pradesh on 01 st  & 02 nd  December, 2025.
texts.text:  Dense fog conditions very likely to prevail during early morning hours in isolated pockets of Himachal Pradesh on 01 st  & 02 nd  December, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Cold wave conditions very likely to prevail in isolated pockets of Punjab on 01 st  & 02 nd December, 2025.
texts.text:  Cold wave conditions very likely to prevail in isolated pockets of Punjab on 01 st  & 02 nd December, 2025.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  27  November  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  161.218  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  87.8%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 107.27 percent of last  year's storage  and 121.44 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  27  November  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  161.218  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  87.8%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 107.27 percent of last  year's storage  and 121.44 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.
texts.text:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against  a  requirement  of  164.05  lakh  quintals, ensuring  a  surplus of  14.60  lakh  quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against  a  requirement  of  164.05  lakh  quintals, ensuring  a  surplus of  14.60  lakh  quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
